name: zai-copilot-chat
description: Bring your own Z.AI API key to VS Code Copilot Chat. Adds Z.AI GLM models through the Language Model Chat Provider API.

zai.temperature.description: Sampling temperature used for chat completions.

zai.maxTokens.description: Optional max output token override. Use 0 to request each Z.AI model's configured maximum.

zai.maxInputTokens.description: Optional context size override advertised to VS Code. Use 0 to advertise each Z.AI model's configured context window.

zai.debugReasoning.description: Write provider reasoning_content to the Z.AI output channel for debugging. Reasoning is not shown in Copilot Chat.

zai.requestTimeout.description: Connection timeout in ms for API requests. During streaming, a separate inactivity timer (half this value, min 90s) aborts if no data arrives. Default 180000 (3 min) — flagship models like glm-5.1 (200K context) need longer cold start than smaller models.

zai.maxRetries.description: Number of automatic retries on transient network errors (fetch failed, timeout, 5xx). Default 2. Set to 0 to disable retries.

zai.showUsageStatusBar.description: Show the latest Z.AI usage summary (prompt→output tokens) in the VS Code status bar after each response.

zai.showQuotaStatusBar.description: Show the Z.AI Coding Plan quota (5-hour / weekly) in the VS Code status bar. Hover for progress bars; click to toggle between windows.

zai.quotaRefreshInterval.description: How often (in minutes) to refresh the Z.AI Coding Plan quota. Set to 0 to disable automatic refresh.

zai.experimentalContextIndicator.description: Experimental: attempt to fill the Copilot Chat context indicator with real Z.AI token usage. Depends on VS Code internals and may break across updates.

zai.research.maxSources.description: Maximum number of sources fetched during a deep research run (`@zai.research /deep`). Lower this to reduce cost and latency.

zai.research.maxIterations.description: Maximum query-expansion iterations before final synthesis. Each iteration searches new queries derived from coverage gaps.

zai.research.concurrency.description: Number of parallel HTTP requests during the search + read phases. Higher is faster but may hit rate limits.

zai.research.cacheTTL.description: Cache time-to-live in seconds for Z.AI search and read results. Set to 0 to disable caching. Default 1 hour.

zai.research.synthesisModel.description: Z.AI model used for planning queries and synthesizing the final research report. Use a high-context model (e.g. glm-5.2 with 1M context) for deep research.

chatParticipants.name: zai
chatParticipants.description: Deep research agent powered by Z.AI Web Search + Web Reader. Iterates queries, fetches many sources, and synthesizes a report with inline citations.

commands.name: deep
commands.description: Maximum depth: 100+ sources, multiple iterations. Slower but thorough.

commands.name: quick
commands.description: Quick mode: 20 sources, 1-2 iterations. Fast and cheap.

languageModelTools.name: zai_webSearch
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search the web using the Z.AI Web Search API. Returns up to 20 results with title, url and a short snippet. The results are intent-optimised for LLM consumption. Use this tool whenever the user asks about fresh internet content, recent events, documentation, or facts you are not certain about. For deeper coverage, call this tool multiple times with different query phrasings, then call zai_webRead on the most relevant URLs.

query.description: Natural-language search query. Be specific and focused; for broad topics, prefer multiple narrow queries.

count.description: Number of results to return. Keep small (5-10) for quick lookups, larger (15-20) for research.

languageModelTools.name: zai_webRead
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read and extract the main content of a single URL using the Z.AI Web Reader API. Returns clean markdown (boilerplate, navigation and ads removed server-side). Use this tool when a search snippet is insufficient and you need the full article text. Always prefer this over asking the user to open a browser.

url.description: Absolute http(s) URL to read. Must include the scheme.

format.description: Output format. 'markdown' preserves headings/links; 'text' is plain prose.
